records.ety: [OE. shade, shadewe, schadewe, AS. sceadu, scead; akin to OS. skado, D. schaduw, OHG. scato, (gen. scatewes), G. schatten, Goth. skadus, Ir. & Gael. sgath, and probably to Gr. sko`tos darkness. √162. Cf. Shadow, Shed a hat.]

defs: Comparative obscurity owing to interception or interruption of the rays of light; partial darkness caused by the intervention of something between the space contemplated and the source of light.
notes: ☞ Shade differs from shadow as it implies no particular form or definite limit; whereas a shadow represents in form the object which intercepts the light. When we speak of the shade of a tree, we have no reference to its form; but when we speak of measuring a pyramid or other object by its shadow, we have reference to its form and extent.

defs: Darkness; obscurity; -- often in the plural.
quotes: The shades of night were falling fast.

defs: An obscure place; a spot not exposed to light; hence, a secluded retreat.
quotes: Let us seek out some desolate shade, and there Weep our sad bosoms empty.

defs: That which intercepts, or shelters from, light or the direct rays of the sun; hence, also, that which protects from heat or currents of air; a screen; protection; shelter; cover; as, a lamp shade.
quotes: The Lord is thy shade upon thy right hand.
Sleep under a fresh tree's shade.
Let the arched knife well sharpened now assail the spreading shades of vegetables.
Envy will merit, as its shade, pursue.

defs: The soul after its separation from the body; -- so called because the ancients it to be perceptible to the sight, though not to the touch; a spirit; a ghost; as, the shades of departed heroes.
quotes: Swift as thought the flitting shade Thro' air his momentary journey made.

defs: The darker portion of a picture; a less illuminated part. See Def. 1, above.
Degree or variation of color, as darker or lighter, stronger or paler; as, a delicate shade of pink.
quotes: White, red, yellow, blue, with their several degrees, or shades and mixtures, as green only in by the eyes.

defs: A minute difference or variation, as of thought, belief, expression, etc.; also, the quality or degree of anything which is distinguished from others similar by slight differences; as, the shades of meaning in synonyms.
quotes: New shades and combinations of thought.
Every shade of religious and political opinion has its own headquarters.

records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Shaded; p. pr. & vb. n. Shading.]

defs: To shelter or screen by intercepting the rays of light; to keep off illumination from.
quotes: I went to crop the sylvan scenes, And shade our altars with their leafy greens.

defs: To shelter; to cover from injury; to protect; to screen; to hide; as, to shade one's eyes.
quotes: Ere in our own house I do shade my head.

defs: To obscure; to dim the brightness of.
quotes: Thou shad'st The full blaze of thy beams.

defs: To pain in obscure colors; to darken.
To mark with gradations of light or color.
To present a shadow or image of; to shadow forth; to represent.
quotes: [The goddess] in her person cunningly did shade That part of Justice which is Equity.

defs: To undergo or exhibit minute difference or variation, as of color, meaning, expression, etc.; to pass by slight changes; -- used chiefly with a preposition, as into, away, off.
quotes: This small group will be most conveniently treated with the emotional division, into which it shades.
